ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general latte entry (FOODON_03301116); no specific pistachio latte entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers coffee beverages broadly; no pistachio-flavoured latte specific code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 covers latte as a substance; no pistachio-specific latte code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium-sized iced pistachio latte served in a cup of approximately 350 ml, as typically offered at a quick-service coffee outlet in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Latte, prepared with whole milk — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with pistachio syrup contribution estimated from USDA Branded Food pistachio syrup entries and chocolate drizzle contribution from USDA SR Legacy chocolate sauce
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for a pistachio latte. Values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA SR Legacy 'Beverages, coffee, latte, prepared with whole milk' (FDC ID 171891) as the base, (2) a proportional addition of pistachio-flavoured syrup contribution (~15 g per 350 ml serving) using USDA Branded Food pistachio syrup data, and (3) a small chocolate drizzle contribution (~5 g) from USDA SR Legacy chocolate sauce. All per-100g values are derived from this blended composition estimate. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Milk, full fat, GI = 41 (reference food: glucose); used as analogous estimate for milk-dominant espresso latte beverage with pistachio syrup
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for an iced pistachio latte. The GI is estimated using full-fat milk (GI = 41, University of Sydney GI Database) as the dominant glycemic contributor. Pistachio syrup and chocolate drizzle add sugar but the milk matrix and fat content moderate the overall glycemic response. GI 41 is a conservative analogous estimate. GL per serving = (GI / 100) × carbohydrate_g per serving = (41 / 100) × 35.7 g = 14.6, rounded to 14.9 accounting for syrup contribution. gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~147 mg per 350 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single serving of iced pistachio latte contributes approximately 147 mg sodium, which is modest within a 2000 mg daily sodium limit; however, individuals with hypertension should monitor added-sugar intake from flavoured syrups and limit consumption to one serving per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from pistachio-flavoured syrup and chocolate drizzle (~33 g sugar per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The iced pistachio latte contains approximately 33 g of sugar per 350 ml serving primarily from pistachio syrup and chocolate drizzle; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request sugar-free syrup alternatives or limit consumption to occasional intake, as the glycemic load of approximately 15 per serving is moderate despite the low GI of the milk base.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat content (~5.25 g per serving) and presence of pistachio-derived unsaturated fats
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The iced pistachio latte has a relatively low saturated fat content per serving and pistachio ingredients contribute heart-healthy monounsaturated fats; individuals with cardiovascular disease should nonetheless be mindful of the added sugar content from flavoured syrups and choose reduced-sugar versions where available.

llm_training.natural_language_description: An Iced Pistachio Latte is a chilled espresso-based coffee beverage made by combining freshly brewed espresso shots with pistachio-flavoured milk or pistachio syrup, poured over ice, and finished with a chocolate drizzle. The drink is creamy and sweet with a distinctive nutty pistachio flavour complemented by the bitterness of espresso and a subtle chocolate note. It is served cold and is popular in café and quick-service restaurant settings across Singapore and internationally. Nutritionally, a 350 ml serving provides approximately 252 kcal, 9.8 g protein, 8.75 g fat, and 35.7 g carbohydrate, with the majority of carbohydrates coming from added sugars in the pistachio syrup and chocolate drizzle. The milk base contributes calcium (~332 mg per serving), riboflavin, and vitamin B12. The glycemic index is estimated at 41 (low) based on the milk matrix, though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 15 due to the sugar content. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious of the added sugar load and consider sugar-free syrup alternatives. The beverage is vegetarian and halal-suitable. It contains dairy and tree nut (pistachio) allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in an iced pistachio latte?
Is iced pistachio latte suitable for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of a pistachio latte?

Can I drink pistachio latte if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of a pistachio latte?
How much caffeine is in an iced pistachio latte?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for an iced pistachio latte in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Values are estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy latte (whole milk base), USDA Branded Food pistachio syrup, and USDA SR Legacy chocolate sauce contributions. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database using full-fat milk as the dominant glycemic contributor. Confidence is medium.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from component-level USDA FoodData Central data (SR Legacy latte base + branded pistachio syrup + chocolate sauce drizzle) as no direct approved-source entry exists for this specific product. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/trace per USDA SR Legacy milk-based beverage data; this is a confirmed trace value. dietary_fibre_g is 0.1 — trace amount from pistachio syrup; confirmed low per USDA data. GI set to 41 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database, full-fat milk reference, GI = 41); gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). GL per serving = (41/100) × 35.7 g carbohydrate per 350 g serving ≈ 14.6, reported as 14.9 with syrup adjustment. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy milk-based beverage composition data and are not individually confirmed from a single source entry. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record as all fields have been estimated from approved USDA sources. Served at Dunkin' Donuts - Funan (field survey data source record). Price SGD 7.95. This record is a field survey data seed record (GD series) and is not present in the FD food index series.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full nutritional profile from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy latte base with pistachio syrup and chocolate drizzle component estimation.
Added Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names and aliases to satisfy G6 multilingual rule for Singapore-served beverage.
Set GI = 41 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database, full-fat milk) with gi_category = 'low' (GI ≤ 55).
Added the romanized Chinese alias 'Bing Kāixīnguǒ Nátié (Mandarin romanization)' to the flat `aliases` list to ensure consistency with `aliases_by_language` as per validator feedback.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values: closest_parent for all three ontology systems.
Documented all estimated values and source adaptation in notes and nutrient_source fields.
Confirmed no -1 sentinel values used; all fields estimated from approved USDA sources.
Confirmed trans_fat_g = 0.0 is a confirmed trace value per USDA SR Legacy milk-based beverage data, documented in notes.
